messages.content:
A(n) __________ is an action, device, procedure, or technique that reduces a threat, a vulnerability, or an attack by eliminating or preventing it, by minimizing the harm it can cause, or by discovering and reporting it so that correct action can be taken.
Group of answer choices

protocol

adversary

attack

countermeasure
countermeasure

A _________ protects against an attack in which one party generates a message for another party to sign.
Group of answer choices

strong hash function

digital signature

data authenticator

weak hash function
digital signature

_________ are analogous to a burglar guessing a safe combination by observing how long it takes to turn the dial from number to number.
Group of answer choices

Timing attacks

Ciphers

Mathematical attacks

Digital standards
Timing attacks

__________ assures that individuals control or influence what information related to them may be collected and stored and by whom and to whom that information may be disclosed.
Group of answer choices

B. Privacy

Data Integrity

System Integrity

Availability
B. Privacy

A threat action in which sensitive data are directly released to an unauthorized entity is __________.
Group of answer choices

exposure

corruption

intrusion

disruption
exposure

_________ attacks have several approaches, all equivalent in effort to factoring the product of two primes.
Group of answer choices

Mathematical

Brute-force

Chosen ciphertext

Timing
Mathematical

A __________ type of attack exploits properties of the RSA algorithm.
Group of answer choices

chosen ciphertext

brute-force

timing

mathematical
mathematical

Combined one byte at a time with the plaintext stream using the XOR operation, a __________ is the output of the pseudorandom bit generator.
Group of answer choices

secure hash

message authentication code

keystream

digital signature
keystream

Digital signatures and key management are the two most important applications of __________ encryption.
Group of answer choices

preimage resistant

advanced

private-key

public-key
public-key

Each individual who is to be included in the database of authorized users must first be __________ in the system.
Group of answer choices

identified

authenticated

verified

enrolled
enrolled

For general-purpose block-oriented transmission you would typically use _______ mode.
Group of answer choices

CBC

CTR

OFB

CFB
CBC (Cipher Block Chaining)

If the analyst is able to get the source system to insert into the system a message chosen by the analyst, then a ________ attack is possible.
Group of answer choices

known-plaintext

chosen ciphertext

chosen-plaintext

chosen text
chosen-plaintext

If the only form of attack that could be made on an encryption algorithm is brute-force, then the way to counter such attacks would be to __________ .
Group of answer choices

use shorter keys

use less keys

use more keys

use longer keys
use longer keys

__________ is an entity capable of accessing objects.
Group of answer choices

object

group

owner

subject
subject
